_meta.description: SSoT Ratchet Registry — centralized module patterns (ADR-294)
_meta.howToAdd: Add module entry under 'modules', then run: npm run ssot:baseline

firestore-collections.description: All Firestore collection/doc names must use COLLECTIONS constant

level-scene-manager-adapter.description: ADR-527 — LevelSceneManagerAdapter must be constructed ONLY through the cached factory createLevelSceneManagerAdapter()/levelSceneManagerFor(). Direct `new LevelSceneManagerAdapter(...)` bypasses the singleton-per-(accessor,level) cache and reintroduces the N-adapter race (each instance had its own scene cache → stale reads). The factory is the single construction site — one long-lived adapter per level (Revit Document model). Migrate any `new` to createLevelSceneManagerAdapter(...) (identical args).

scene-manager-adapter-hook.description: ADR-577 — the memoized `getSceneManager` builder that binds an ISceneManager to the current level lives ONCE as the useSceneManagerAdapter() hook. The identical `const getSceneManager = useCallback(() => { if (!levelManager.currentLevelId) return null; return createLevelSceneManagerAdapter(levelManager.getLevelScene, levelManager.setLevelScene, levelManager.currentLevelId); }, [levelManager])` block was copy-pasted verbatim into 20 interactive tool hooks (copy/move/mirror/rotation/scale/trim/stretch/offset/fillet/chamfer/extend/array/entity-clipboard + the wall-pick + array-polar/path + body-drag tools). Re-declaring a `const getSceneManager = useCallback(...)` reintroduces the duplicate — call `const getSceneManager = useSceneManagerAdapter(levelManager)` instead (the SSoT hook, which delegates to the ADR-527 cached factory). The SSoT file is allowlisted only for its doc-comment example of the forbidden shape.

nan-safe-box3-bounds.description: ADR-537 — every 3D bounds-from-object computation MUST go through finiteBox3FromObject() in bim-3d/scene/finite-bounds.ts. A raw `new THREE.Box3().setFromObject(obj)` + `.isEmpty()` is NaN-BLIND (`isEmpty()` is `max < min`, which is `false` when a bound is NaN), so a single corrupt vertex yields a box that reports 'not empty' yet carries NaN bounds. Fed into the SHARED camera frame (viewport.frameBounds) or the section box, that NaN blanks the ENTIRE viewport — both the DXF underlay AND the lit BIM. finiteBox3FromObject() returns null on empty OR non-finite so callers hit their existing no-op branch. It is the ONLY legitimate `setFromObject` site. Route new bounds sites through it (null-guard per local semantics), never inline. Test files are exempt (they assert on raw bounds).

grip-settings-schema.description: ADR-559 — the grip-settings object SHAPE is defined ONCE in types/grip-settings-schema.ts (GripSettingsBase + GripStyleExtras + GripColors/ResolvedGripColors + GripSettingsFull). Every other grip-settings type (domain GripSettings, GripStyle, the input DTO, MockGripSettings, preview prop-types) MUST be a PROJECTION (`type X = ... & Omit<...>` / re-export), not a standalone `interface GripSettings`/`GripStyle`/`MockGripSettings`. Re-declaring one reintroduces the 5+ divergent-copies duplication where adding one field meant editing every copy by hand. Add new grip fields to GripSettingsBase. Local view-models with extra display-only fields must use a distinct name (e.g. GripSettingsSummary / GripCssPreviewInput), not `GripSettings`.

grip-factory-defaults.description: ADR-559 §3b / ADR-445 — the grip default VALUES (aperture 20, warm=GRIP_WARM_COLOR magenta/ροζ, sentinel cold) live ONCE in config/grip-factory-defaults.ts. Before this they were re-declared in 7 divergent places (FACTORY_DEFAULTS GRIP_DEFAULTS, types/gripSettings DEFAULT_GRIP_SETTINGS, GripStyleStore INITIAL, settings-core/defaults DEFAULT_GRIP_SETTINGS, domain.validateGripSettings inline defaults, useUnifiedSpecificSettings preview mock, rendering/grips/constants DEFAULT_GRIP_COLORS) that disagreed on apertureSize (10 vs 20) and warm colour (orange vs hot-pink) — an inconsistency that made hover grips render orange while the store said 20. Every default surface now DERIVES (`...GRIP_FACTORY_DEFAULTS`) from the canonical. A new `apertureSize:<number>` literal means a NEW divergent default object — add it to the canonical instead. The only allowlisted non-canonical site is the GripSettings.tsx Small/Medium/Large preset buttons (deliberately distinct preset values, not defaults). Mode-variant deltas (draft/hover/selection sizes) carry no apertureSize and are unaffected. Warm colour discipline is enforced by the single GRIP_WARM_COLOR SSoT constant + the grip-factory-defaults-ssot.test.ts parity test, not a content pattern here (GRIP_WARM_COLOR is legitimately referenced by mode-deltas).

line-settings-schema.description: ADR-559 §3e — the line-settings object SHAPE is defined ONCE in types/line-settings-schema.ts (LineSettingsBase + the LineType/LineCapStyle/LineJoinStyle unions). Every other line-settings type (domain LineSettings, the LinePreview/CurrentSettingsDisplay preview view-models) MUST be a PROJECTION (`type X = Pick<LineSettingsBase, ...> & {...}`), not a standalone `interface LineSettings`. Re-declaring one reintroduces the 4-divergent-copies duplication where adding one field meant editing every copy by hand. Add new line fields to LineSettingsBase. The tiny CSS-preview input `{color,width,style}` (different field names/types) must use a distinct name (LineCssPreviewInput), not `LineSettings` — mirror the grip GripCssPreviewInput.

text-settings-schema.description: ADR-559 §3f — the text-settings object SHAPE is defined ONCE in types/text-settings-schema.ts (TextSettingsBase + the TextAlign/TextBaseline unions). Every other text-settings type (domain TextSettings, the TextSettingsContext public type, the LinePreview/CurrentSettingsDisplay preview view-models) MUST be a PROJECTION (`type X = Pick<TextSettingsBase, ...>`), not a standalone `interface TextSettings`. Re-declaring one reintroduces the 5-divergent-copies duplication. Add new text fields to TextSettingsBase. The tiny CSS-preview input (whose fontWeight is a CSS string union, not the numeric 100-900 weight) must use a distinct name (TextCssPreviewInput), not `TextSettings`.

ortho-delta.description: ADR-507 — the ORTHO/F8 dominant-axis delta clamp lives ONCE in bim/grips/ortho-delta.ts as constrainDeltaToDominantAxis() (frame-agnostic pure). It was byte-identical private copies (quantizeToDominantAxis/applyOrtho) across 5+ grip files (floor-finish/slab/slab-opening/rect-grip-engine/hatch). Re-declaring a private quantize*DominantAxis reintroduces the drift — import constrainDeltaToDominantAxis instead.

point-translate-helpers.description: ADR-090 — point+vector translation lives ONCE in geometry-vector-utils.ts: translatePoint/translatePoints (z-PRESERVING 2D add), translatePoint3D/translatePoints3D (Firestore-safe 3D, z := z ?? 0), addPoint3D (genuine 3D z-SUM). Big players keep ONE `add` per dimension (three.js Vector2/Vector3.add, glMatrix vec2/vec3.add); offsetPoint (=addScaledVector) and subtractPoints stay separate. It had been re-implemented as 13 divergent named copies (applyDelta ×2, shiftPoint3D ×2, shiftPolygon3D, translateVertex ×5, plus private translatePoint/translate/translate3D/offsetPoint-2arg) + ~140 inline `{ x: p.x+delta.x, y: p.y+delta.y }` literals across ~90 files (2026-07-06 consolidation). Re-declaring `function applyDelta|shiftPoint3D|shiftPolygon3D|translateVertex` reintroduces the drift — import the canonical instead. NOTE: inline-literal discipline is enforced by the canonical + code review + ADR-090, NOT by a content pattern here — CHECK 3.7 is repo-wide with no path scoping, so a generic `{x:a.x+b.x,y:a.y+b.y}` ban would over-block legitimate non-dxf vector math and every thin delegator. This zero-tolerance name guard locks the specific eliminated duplicates.

inherit-entity-style.description: ADR-510 Phi5 — style inheritance onto a derived primitive (explode/offset/fillet keep source layer/colour/lineweight/linetype) lives ONCE as inheritEntityStyle() + ENTITY_STYLE_SKIP in systems/entity-creation/inherit-entity-style.ts. It was inlined as a private inheritEntityStyle + ARC_STYLE_SKIP in fillet-curve-geometry.ts. Re-declaring a local skip-set or inheritEntityStyle reintroduces the duplicate — import the SSoT (its skip-set is a superset, safe for line/arc/circle/rect sources).

grip-kind-index.description: ADR-510 Phi3c — parsing the trailing numeric index of a `<prefix>-N` grip kind lives ONCE as parseGripKindIndex() in systems/grip/grip-kind-index.ts (lastIndexOf-based, returns null on malformed/negative). It was inline `parseInt(gripKind.slice(prefix.length), 10)` across 7 grip files (slab/roof/floor-finish/slab-opening/mep-underfloor/column/wall) and backed parsePolylineSegIndex. Import parseGripKindIndex; keep any domain-specific lower bound (e.g. wall idx>=1) as an extra check at the call site.

scalar-clamp.description: TIER A duplicate-audit (2026-07-08) / ADR-071 — the scalar clamp family (clamp/clamp01/clamp255) lives ONCE in the neutral, dependency-free utils/scalar-math.ts; rendering/entities/shared/geometry-utils.ts re-exports it for backward-compat. Before this ~61 sites across config/bim/bim-3d/snapping/systems/rendering hand-rolled inline `Math.max(0, Math.min(1, v))` / `Math.max(0, Math.min(255, v))` clamps and local clamp01/clamp255 re-declarations. Import clamp/clamp01/clamp255 from utils/scalar-math instead of re-declaring. GUARD SCOPE NOTE (mirrors point-translate-helpers): CHECK 3.7 is repo-wide with no path scoping, and scalar-math is a dxf-viewer util that non-dxf code cannot import — so the generic shapes that legitimately occur in other domains are deliberately NOT guarded here: the forward `Math.max(0, Math.min(1,` inline (report-engine/address-editor/floorplan-background), the reverse `Math.min(1, Math.max(0,` inline (procurement/comparison-service), and the generic `function clamp(` / `const clamp = (` (report-engine/address-editor). Those forms stay enforced by the canonical + code review + jscpd (N.18), exactly like ADR-090 inline-literal discipline. The patterns below are the collision-safe subset: the eliminated helper NAMES (clamp01/clamp255 as function- or const-declarations) plus the clamp255 inline shape, which appear only in dxf plus one non-dxf clamp01 (floorplan-background.service.ts, a separate-domain clamp that cannot import the dxf util — allowlisted). Allowlisted dxf survivors: heat-load-color.ts clamp01 (NaN-guard wrapper delegating to clampUnitInterval), studio-background-texture.ts clamp255 (rounds-before-clamp, different behaviour), and geometry-utils.ts (the backward-compat re-export shim). The AxisCutSliderControl.tsx clamp(v, range) (domain-range signature) and RibbonMepCircuitConductorsWidget.tsx clamp(n) (fixed-bounds closure) use the un-guarded generic shapes, so they need no allowlist entry.

normalize-angle-deg.description: TIER A duplicate-audit (2026-07-08) — normalizing a degree value into [0,360) lives ONCE as normalizeAngleDeg() in rendering/entities/shared/geometry-angle-utils.ts (branch form: `let n = deg % 360; if (n < 0) n += 360;`). It had been re-implemented as the double-modulo idiom `((x % 360) + 360) % 360` inline + local re-declarations across ~18 sites (io/tek, formatting, dimensions, array path-sampler, polygon-azimuth-utils, dxf-symbol-detector, thermal, snapping intersection-calculators, clip-geometry, useCalibration, …). Re-introducing either the double-modulo inline or a local `function normalizeAngleDeg` forks the wrap logic — import normalizeAngleDeg instead. Both patterns are zero-collision repo-wide. Allowlist: geometry-angle-utils.ts (the SSoT), and radial-ring-logic.ts for the single legitimate self-use of the double-modulo inside pieSectorPath() (a pure SVG-arc large-arc-flag test, not an entity-angle normalization).

safe-storage.description: TIER A duplicate-audit (2026-07-08) / ADR-092 — SSR-safe localStorage access lives ONCE in utils/storage-utils.ts (storageGet/storageSet/storageRemove/storageHas over the private isStorageAvailable() guard + STORAGE_KEYS registry). Three persistence services (layer-state-persistence, layer-filter-persistence, layer-picker-persistence) had each re-declared a verbatim `function safeStorage(): Storage | null { ... }` plus hand-rolled getItem/setItem try/catch guards that bypassed the centralized SSR + quota handling. Use the storageGet/Set/Remove/Has helpers instead of re-declaring a safeStorage accessor. The `function safeStorage` name is zero-collision repo-wide.

edge-triggered-tool-lifecycle.description: TIER A duplicate-audit (2026-07-08) / ADR-589 — the transition-only tool lifecycle idiom (a `wasActiveRef = useRef(false)` cell + a 3-branch effect that fires the on-enter body only on the false->true edge, the on-exit body only on the true->false edge, then tail-assigns `wasActiveRef.current = isActive`, deps `[isActive]`) lives ONCE in useEdgeTriggeredLifecycle(isActive, onActivate, onDeactivate). It had been byte-copied across 13 modify-tool hooks (trim/extend/fillet/chamfer/offset/array/array-path/array-polar/wall-attach/wall-split/schedule-region-pick/stretch/wall-pick-scaffold). Re-declaring an inline `wasActiveRef = useRef` re-implements the edge detector — call useEdgeTriggeredLifecycle instead. It is NOT the same as useToolLifecycle (ADR-363), which is deliberately NOT transition-only (it fires activate()/deactivate() on every deps change + deactivate() on-mount, used by the drawing tools). The `wasActiveRef = useRef` shape is zero-collision repo-wide (grep-verified 2026-07-08). Allowlist: useEdgeTriggeredLifecycle.ts (the SSoT) + systems/tools/useModifyToolActivation.ts (ADR-577 deselect->pick FSM — a DIFFERENT mechanism that legitimately keeps its own internal wasActiveRef edge-latch).

create-confirm-store.description: ADR-507 Phi3 — Promise-handshake confirm-dialog stores (open dialog with payload then await the user choice as a Promise) live on the createConfirmStore<TState,TAction>() factory. The _state/_pendingResolve/_subs/_notify boilerplate was hand-copied byte-identical across 7 stores (column-perimeter/column-adopt-size/shear-wall-extent/column-becomes-wall/section-relationship/wall-cascade-delete/bim-family-type-delete). Declaring a new `let _pendingResolve` re-implements the handshake — call createConfirmStore(closedState) plus thin named wrappers instead.

tek-color-to-hex.description: ADR-526/531 (Tekton .TEK IMPORT) — Tekton `<color>` (RGB hex, no `#`) -> canonical scene `#RRGGBB` lives on tekColorToHex() in io/tek/tek-color.ts. It reuses the export-side SSoT colorHex6 (normalize/uppercase/strip/fallback in export/core/tek/tek-xml-writer.ts) and adds ONLY the import-side `#` prefix the renderer/CSS require (strokeStyle without `#` is invalid -> black/invisible line). The `#${colorHex6(raw)}` wrapper was hand-copied byte-identical as a local tekColorToHex in tek-primitive-to-scene.ts + tek-structural-to-scene.ts. Writing a new inline `#${colorHex6(...)}` re-implements the wrapper — import { tekColorToHex } from './tek-color' instead. (colorHex6 itself stays the export SSoT; this guards only the `#`-prefixed import form.)

create-tool-bridge-store.description: ADR-404 Phi5b/5c — drawing-mode <-> ribbon/3D handle bridge stores (a module-level THandle|null cell a tool hook publishes and a ribbon/3D reader consumes via get()/use()) live on the createToolBridgeStore<THandle>() factory. The `let handle: XToolBridgeHandle | null` + Set<Listener> + emit/subscribe/getSnapshot/getServerSnapshot + set/get/use boilerplate was hand-copied byte-identical across 16 stores (column/foundation/railing/furniture/electrical-panel/floorplan-symbol/wall/slab/beam + 7 mep). Declaring a new module-level `let handle: <X>ToolBridgeHandle` re-implements the bridge cell — call createToolBridgeStore<XHandle>() instead (the factory delegates the pub/sub machinery to createExternalStore and bundles the useSyncExternalStore use() binding).

create-external-store.description: WAVE 2/2.5/2.6/2.7 (Opus 4.8) — zero-React single-value pub/sub stores (a module-level `let state` + `const listeners = new Set<() => void>()` + notify/get/set/subscribe, consumed via useSyncExternalStore) live on the createExternalStore<T>(initial,{equals?}) factory. The listener-set + _notify boilerplate was hand-copied byte-identical across ~100+ stores. WAVE 2/2.5 caught the `new Set<() => void>` literal form (~22 stores); WAVE 2.6 caught the bare-alias form — `type Listener = () => void` (or `Subscriber`) + `new Set<Listener>()` / `private listeners: Set<Listener> = new Set()` — across ~43+ more stores; WAVE 2.7 caught the DOMAIN-NAMED alias form — `type XListener = (...) => void` + `new Set<XListener>()` / `: Set<XListener> = new Set()` (ViewportListener/RegistryListener/ModalPresenceListener/IsolateEffectsListener/StoreListener/RoofEdgeListener/ResolutionListener/ReadoutListener/DrawingMachineListener/CommandHistoryListener…) — migrating 14 more stores (ModalPresence/IsolateEffects/mep-wire-waypoint/roof-edge-selection/guide-drag/line-style-registry+dim-style-registry version-signal/guide-store+construction-point-store+CommandHistory version-signal/DrawingStateMachine FSM/bim-edge-resolution/Bim3DCursorReadout/ViewportStore 2-instance). The guard pattern is therefore `new Set<\w*Listener>` / `new Set<\w*Subscriber>` / `: Set<\w*Listener>` (the `\w*` covers both the bare `Listener`/`Subscriber` and every domain-named alias). Declaring a new such listener set re-implements the pub/sub cell — call createExternalStore<T>() plus thin domain-named wrappers (version-signal `createExternalStore<number>(0)` for multi-map/registry/hot-path stores that keep their Maps/stacks as mutation accelerators; a `lastEvent` side-channel + wrapper for payload-carrying `subscribe` signatures). ALLOWLIST = the 3 sibling store factories (canonical homes: external/confirm/toggle) + the genuine NOT-A-FIT stores that legitimately cannot use a single-value store: async caches (material-thumbnail-resolver / opening-tag-style-service), a per-level Map cache (envelope-spec-store), the ADR-040 multi-cell hot-path stores (ImmediateSnapStore / ImmediateTransformStore / ImmediatePositionStore 3-set / HoverStore 2-set), the per-key signature-cache slice store (RibbonFieldStore, ADR-547), the WAVE 2.6 NOT-A-FIT stores (rotation-snap-store multi-field factory-singleton try/catch; regression-alert-bus pure event bus zero state; CanvasNumericInputStore imperative + _dde; SnapOverrideOrchestrator 3 independent state machines), and the WAVE 2.7 NOT-A-FIT stores: clash-focus-bus + aria-live-bus (pure fire-and-forget announce buses, payload-carrying listener + zero readable state), device-pixel-ratio (payload callback + media-query lifecycle, no snapshot), KeyboardFocusManager (payload-carrying subscribe + two channels broadcasting ONE shared value + disposed-lifecycle guard). WAVE 2.8 caught the INLINE parametrized-fn form — `new Set<(x: Payload) => void>` / `: Set<(x: Payload) => void> = new Set()` (payload-carrying listener, not the bare `() => void`) — migrating globalStores (Grid+Ruler single-state, payload→wrapper) + CanvasSettings (version-signal, this.settings stays SSoT); guard patterns `new Set<([^)]+) => void>` + `: Set<([^)]+) => void> = new Set`. WAVE 2.8 NOT-A-FIT (allowlisted): UnifiedFrameScheduler (ADR-040 RAF per-frame FrameMetrics fan-out), geometry-rendering-utils BaseConfigurationManager (the dedup SSoT base itself — 1 subclass CursorConfiguration in cursor/ ADR-040, no stored state), SyncService (cross-tab BroadcastChannel event-bus, transient message payload + zero readable state). StableFirestoreProvider stays a per-key Map<key,{callbacks:Set}> subscription multiplexer (instantiated as untyped `new Set([cb])`, not matched). Test files are exempt via exemptPatterns. WAVE 3 (Opus 4.8 orchestrator) PROMOTED the factory out of the dxf subapp to the shared platform lib `src/lib/state/createExternalStore.ts` (big-player canonical: a core store primitive belongs at package/core level, consumed everywhere via useSyncExternalStore — Zustand/Redux/Jotai vanilla); the old dxf path `src/subapps/dxf-viewer/stores/createExternalStore.ts` is now a thin `export * from '@/lib/state/createExternalStore'` re-export shim (all ~126 dxf import sites unchanged). This let the 5 non-dxf hand-rolled pub/sub stores migrate onto the shared SSoT: useFirestoreBuildings + super-admin-active-company (Firestore single-state `new Set<() => void>`), addressEditorMachine (FSM `new Set<(state)=>void>`), GeoCanvasConfig (config single-state `new Set<(config)=>void>`), useFloorsByBuilding (`new Set<FloorsListener>`). WAVE 3 NOT-A-FIT (allowlisted): CanvasRegistry (2-arg `(event: string, data) => void` event bus, zero readable state — buses stay bespoke, same call as clash-focus-bus/aria-live-bus/regression-alert-bus).

browser-performance-memory.description: ADR-546 — The non-standard Chrome `performance.memory` API must be declared and read ONLY through src/lib/platform/browser-performance-memory.ts. Re-declaring `interface PerformanceWithMemory`/`PerformanceMemory` or casting `as PerformanceWithMemory` anywhere else reintroduces the 4-type + 12-reader cross-subapp duplication with inconsistent units (bytes vs MB) and unsafe non-optional casts. Use readPerformanceMemory() (raw bytes) or readCpuMemoryMb() (MB). Type re-exports via `export type { ... } from` are allowed.

gcs-buckets.description: GCS bucket names and GCP project ID must use gcs-buckets.ts constants

storage-public-upload.description: Server-side publicly-accessible uploads must go through uploadPublicFile(). Three failure modes blocked: (1) makePublic() silently no-ops on UBLA-enabled buckets making storage.googleapis.com URLs return 403; (2) hardcoded storage.googleapis.com URL templates assume public ACL; (3) bucket.file().save() without prior FILES/{fileId} pre-claim races against onStorageFinalize orphan-cleanup which deletes the file hundreds of ms after upload (ADR-327 incident 2026-04-27). The pre-claim must be written BEFORE save() — uploadPublicFile() does this atomically.

enterprise-id.description: All document IDs must use EnterpriseIdService — no crypto.randomUUID()

domain-constants.description: Entity types, sender types, participant roles must use DOMAIN_CONSTANTS

entity-code-field.description: useEntityCodeSuggestion hook must only be imported/called from EntityCodeField — sealed abstraction (ADR-233). parseFloorLevel utility from the same module is allowed everywhere.

intent-badge-utils.description: Intent/confidence badge variants must use centralized intent-badge-utils

notification-keys.description: Notification i18n keys must be imported from NOTIFICATION_KEYS (SSoT). Domain hooks in src/hooks/notifications/ own the when-to-fire logic. Callers must never hardcode i18n key strings inside notifications.success|error|info|warning() — use a domain hook (useContactNotifications, useProjectNotifications) that references NOTIFICATION_KEYS. Hardcoded Greek/English literals inside those calls are a double violation (SSoT + i18n SOS N.11).

addDoc-prohibition.description: NEVER use addDoc()/.add()/.doc() — always setDoc() with enterprise ID

tenant-company-id.description: Company ID must come from TENANT_COMPANY_ID/getCompanyId() — never hardcoded

deletion-registry.description: Deletion dependencies must be declared in DELETION_REGISTRY — no inline dependency arrays

soft-delete-config.description: Soft-delete entity config must use centralized SOFT_DELETE_CONFIG

entity-code-config.description: Entity type codes (DI/GK/ST/ME/etc) must come from entity-code-config — no hardcoded 2-char codes

openai-provider.description: OpenAI provider creation must use getOpenAIProvider() — no inline createOpenAI() calls outside the SSoT module

ai-role-access-matrix.description: AI role permissions must use AI_ROLE_ACCESS_MATRIX — no inline access configs

environment-security.description: Security policies must use ENVIRONMENT_SECURITY_CONFIG — no inline security rules

file-upload-limits.description: File size limits must come from FILE_TYPE_CONFIG — no hardcoded byte calculations

file-type-classification.description: AI classification + preview + body-text extraction decisions for file types must go through classification-registry.ts (ADR-296). Before ADR-296 the same MIME list lived hand-synchronised in three places (route.ts, useFileClassification.ts, preview-registry.ts) and drift caused ~10 fix commits on 2026-04-21. Forbidden patterns (below): (1) re-declaring CLASSIFIABLE_TYPES / CLASSIFIABLE_EXTS / IMAGE_MIME_TYPES / TEXT_MIME_TYPES / DXF_EXTENSIONS outside the registry — every consumer must import `isAIClassifiable`, `getPreviewType`, `getFileCategory`, `getMediaDocumentType`, or `getExtractorKind`. (2) re-declaring the DOCX_MIME / XLSX_MIME / DXF_MIMES / SVG_MIME constants that used to drive the extractor switch — dispatch goes through `getExtractorKind` + `runExtractor`.

scene-units.description: SceneUnits type + mm⇄scene-units conversion + $INSUNITS resolution + bounds-diagonal heuristic. Single source of truth for any tool that builds geometry from mm-baked defaults (ADR-358 stair tool Phase 8; future tools must reuse instead of redefining unit conversion locally). Previously: dxf-scene-builder hardcoded scene.units='mm' regardless of $INSUNITS and stair-completion duplicated the conversion table — fixed 2026-05-17.

stair-presets-service.description: Stair library presets CRUD (ADR-358 G26, Q32) must go through StairPresetsService — no direct collection() writes, no inline `addDoc` on stair_presets, no inline preset ID generation. Path migrated to bim/ per ADR-363 Phase 0.5 (2026-05-19).

stair-firestore-service.description: Stair persistence + soft-lock (ADR-358 Phase 8, G24) must go through StairFirestoreService — no direct collection() writes on floorplan_stairs, no inline addDoc, no inline stair ID generation, no direct onSnapshot bypass of firestoreQueryService SSoT subscribe wrapper. Path migrated to bim/ per ADR-363 Phase 0.5 (2026-05-19).

bim-folder-residency.description: ADR-363 Phase 0.5 — all BIM entity code (stair / wall / opening / slab / column / beam / slab-opening) MUST reside under src/subapps/dxf-viewer/bim/. Legacy paths systems/stairs/, types/stair, rendering/entities/StairRenderer, hooks/data/useStairPersistence, ui/ribbon/hooks/useRibbonStairBridge are removed (2026-05-19); imports targeting any of those paths are forbidden — use the bim/ canonical path instead (bim/stairs/, bim/types/stair-types, bim/renderers/StairRenderer, bim/hooks/use-stair-persistence, bim/hooks/use-ribbon-stair-bridge).

showcase-core.description: Public-showcase orchestration (PDF pipeline, email pipeline, public payload/PDF routes, snapshot builder, share resolver, email builder, PDF service+renderer) must use the showcase-core factories (ADR-321). New showcase surfaces must delegate to createShowcasePdfRoute, createShowcaseEmailRoute, createPublicShowcasePayloadRoute, createPublicShowcasePdfRoute, createShowcaseSnapshotBuilder, createShowcaseShareResolver, createShowcaseEmailBuilder, ShowcasePDFService, BaseShowcaseRenderer from src/services/showcase-core/ — not reimplement the orchestration.

notification-events.description: Notification events/channels/severities must use centralized registry

project-mutation-impact.description: Project mutation fields/guards must use PROJECT_MUTATION_* constants

ai-pipeline-config.description: Pipeline queue/timeout/confidence configs must use PIPELINE_*_CONFIG constants

feature-flags.description: Feature flags must use APP_FEATURE_FLAGS/isAppFeatureEnabled — no inline flag objects

business-hours.description: Business hours/slots must use BUSINESS_HOURS config — no hardcoded scheduling values

audit-tracked-fields.description: Audit trail field definitions must use *_TRACKED_FIELDS from centralized config

user-settings-repository.description: Per-user UI settings (cursor / rulers-grid / dxf line+text+grip / snap modes) are owned by `userSettingsRepository` (UserSettings SSoT — Firestore-backed industry pattern). Every read on USER_PREFERENCES must go through `userSettingsRepository.subscribeSlice()` / `getSlice()` and every write through `userSettingsRepository.updateSlice()` (debounced 500ms autosave + Zod validation + audit trail via EntityAuditService). Direct addDoc/setDoc/updateDoc/onSnapshot calls against `user_preferences` outside the allowlist are forbidden — they bypass the schema validator, the migration pipeline, and the audit trail.

cad-toggles.description: CAD toolbar toggles (osnap/grid/snap/ortho/polar/dynInput) must use useCadToggles — persisted via userSettingsRepository slice 'dxfViewer.cadToggles'. No duplicate useState-only toggle implementations.

modules._comment_adr377: === TIER 3 — ADR-377 (2026-06-03): BIM subcategories taxonomy + line-pattern SSoT ===

bim-subcategories.description: ADR-377 — BIM subcategory taxonomy + per-subcategory line patterns are a closed SSoT. The per-category subcategory keys (SUBCATEGORY_TAXONOMY), the wired-vs-stub gate (WIRED_SUBCATEGORIES / isWiredSubcategory), the lookup helper (getAllSubcategoryKeysForCategory), and the line-pattern catalog (BIM_LINE_PATTERNS / BUILT_IN_DASH_ARRAYS / linePatternToDashArray) live ONLY in config/bim-subcategories.ts and config/bim-line-patterns.ts. The 2D resolver (resolveSubcategoryStyle) and the 3D edge resolver (resolve3DEdgeStyle + buildEdgeOverlay + attachEdgesProjection) both read the same objectStyles[cat].subcategories SSoT — they must consume these tables, not re-declare them. Re-exports (export { X }) are allowed; re-declaring any of these constants/functions outside the canonical files is forbidden (would fork the taxonomy and desync 2D↔3D styling).

entity-audit-trail.description: Entity audit trail is a closed SSoT (ADR-195 Phase 10). Server writes go through EntityAuditService.recordChange(). Client reads go through EntityAuditClientService.subscribeGlobal()/subscribeEntity(), which compose firestoreQueryService. The canonical hooks are useEntityAudit + useGlobalAuditTrail. Forbidden (reader-side, enforced by regex below): direct writes to entity_audit_trail, inline collection() queries on ENTITY_AUDIT_TRAIL, re-implementation of either hook, manual RealtimeService event lists like GLOBAL_AUDIT_WATCH_EVENTS/ENTITY_EVENT_MAP, or bypassing firestoreQueryService with raw onSnapshot on the collection. Writer-side coverage (positive requirement — any write to a tracked entity must also call recordChange) is enforced by CHECK 3.17 via scripts/check-entity-audit-coverage.js + .entity-audit-coverage-baseline.json. The two together guarantee the audit trail is both untamperable (reader gate) and complete (writer gate).

dxf-raster-generator.description: Server-side DXF scene → PNG rasterisation (ADR-312 Phase 3). Canonical entry point for every surface that needs a DXF thumbnail: Cloud Function trigger (functions/src/storage/dxf-thumbnail-onfinalize.ts), migration endpoint (/api/admin/migrate-dxf-thumbnails), and any future reader. Uses @resvg/resvg-js (pure WASM) to stay serverless-safe. Inline `new Resvg(...)` outside the allowlist = forbidden: we must not scatter SVG→PNG calls across the codebase, or the thumbnail size/background/padding drift from the browser-side DXF Viewer. The functions/shared mirror is the SSoT mirror kept byte-for-byte in sync.

decode-processed-dxf-json.description: gzip-aware decoder for `.dxf.processed.json` payloads (ADR-033 writer uses pako.gzip). Any server-side consumer of the processed JSON must go through this helper so the gzip magic-byte handling stays in one place. Regression pin: 2026-04-18 — the initial ADR-312 Phase 3 back-fill assumed plain UTF-8 and failed on every existing processed file. The functions/shared mirror is kept byte-for-byte in sync.

dxf-thumbnail-selfheal.description: Server-side self-heal orchestrator for the DXF→thumbnail pipeline (ADR-312 Phase 7.3). ensureDxfThumbnail(fileId) is the single entry point for any request-path code that needs to nudge a DXF through the missing pipeline stage (`.processed.json` generation via processDxf + FileRecord patch, or direct rasterize via rasterizeDxfThumbnail). Used fire-and-forget by the public showcase route on each linked parking/storage DXF, and synchronously by the back-fill endpoint helpers. Forbidden elsewhere: inline ensureProcessedDataForDxf / rasterizeDxfThumbnail reimplementations — every new consumer must import from this module.

property-media-reader.description: Reading property-linked media (photos, floorplans) from the `files` collection on the server must go through PropertyMediaService (listPropertyMedia / downloadPropertyMedia / countPropertyMedia). The service centralises the canonical filter set (companyId + entityType=property + entityId + category), active-lifecycle pruning, createdAt sorting, and jsPDF mime allowlisting for server-side PDF embedding. Forbidden: inline `.collection(COLLECTIONS.FILES).where('entityType','==','property')...` pipelines outside the service — each duplicate is a chance for tenant-isolation or lifecycle drift. ADR-312 (Property Showcase), ADR-031 (File Storage).

firestore-realtime.description: Firestore realtime subscriptions must go through the canonical service: firestoreQueryService.subscribe / subscribeDoc / subscribeSubcollection (ADR-214 + ADR-355 consolidation). Post-ADR-361, the service enforces an automatic content-equality guard on every emission (dequal deep equal default, EqualitySlot with super-admin switcher reset, skipEqualityGuard + equalityFn opt-outs) — direct onSnapshot() calls bypass this guard and are forbidden outside the allowlist. The SSoT comparator module lives at `src/services/firestore/firestore-equality.ts`.

modules._comment_tier8: === TIER 8: ADR-314 PHASE A — CORE SSoT MODULES (2026-04-18) ===

enterprise-id-convenience.description: All generate*Id() named functions must be declared ONLY in enterprise-id.service.ts (core) or enterprise-id-convenience.ts (barrel). No local wrappers/duplicates. Allowlist covers the sealed SSoT set + the React hook barrel (useEnterpriseIds) + the server-side composite-key ID generators (id-generation.ts — different purpose). ADR-314 Phase A.

intl-formatting.description: Intl API wrappers (formatDate/formatDateTime/formatRelativeTime/formatCurrency/formatNumber/formatPercentage/formatPriceWithUnit/formatList/sortByLocale/compareByLocale/isRTLLocale/getTextDirection) must be declared ONLY in intl-formatting.ts. Phase A baseline freezes scattered local formatters; Phase B/C migrate callers. ADR-314 Phase A.

intl-domain.description: Domain-specific Intl wrappers (formatFloorLabel/formatFloorString/getCategoryLabel/getStatusLabel/getPricePerSqmUnit/formatDateShort/formatDateLong/formatDateForDisplay/formatDateGreek/formatCurrencyWhole/formatCurrencyCompact/getDaysUntilCompletion/formatFlexibleDateTime/formatFlexibleDate/formatFlexibleTimeOnly) must be declared ONLY in intl-domain.ts. ADR-314 Phase A.

date-local.description: Date normalization + ISO/millis conversion canonical module. Blocks: (a) re-declaration of normalizeToDate/normalizeToISO/fieldToISO/normalizeToMillis/getNestedTimestampISO/toLocalDateInputValue/combineLocalDateTime; (b) scattered new Date().toISOString() — migrated to nowISO() in Phase C.1; (c) scattered Timestamp.fromDate(new Date()) — migrated to nowTimestamp() in Phase C.2. Baseline freezes current 309 + 19 legacy occurrences. ADR-314 Phase A + C.

design-system.description: Design system helpers (getTypographyClass/getSpacingClass/getComponentSizeClass/getInteractiveStateClass/getGridClass/getShadowClass/getBorderRadiusClass/getResponsiveClass/getStatusBadgeClass/getFormFieldClass) must be declared ONLY in design-system.ts. NOTE: getStatusColor in design-system is the SEMANTIC-TOKEN variant (success/error/info → CSS vars); the DOMAIN-STATUS variant lives in status-helpers (ADR-314 Phase B) and is enforced separately. Allowlist covers the canonical re-alias site in property-status-core.

ui-tooltip.description: All tooltip UI must use @/components/ui/tooltip (Radix wrapper, dark bg + white text SSoT). Direct @radix-ui/react-tooltip imports are forbidden outside the canonical wrapper. Use Tooltip/TooltipTrigger/TooltipContent/TooltipProvider or InfoTooltip from @/components/ui/InfoTooltip.

modules._comment_tier8_phaseB: === TIER 8 cont. — ADR-314 PHASE B (2026-04-18): canonical domain status SSoT ===

status-helpers.description: Domain-aware status helpers — getStatusColor(domain, status, opts)/getStatusLabel(domain, status, opts)/getStatusIcon(domain, status) — discriminated union over StatusDomain ('storage'|'obligation'|'lead'|'communication'|'buildingTimeline'|'buildingProject'|'project'|'property'). Canonical SSoT for ALL exported domain status helpers; previously scattered across obligations-utils.ts, project-utils.ts (deleted), leads/utils/formatters.ts (deleted), projects/structure-tab/utils/status.ts (deleted), and 4 component-local copies. The semantic-token variant (success/error/info) remains in design-system.ts (orthogonal namespace, allowlisted). Property-status alias in property-status-core.ts is allowlisted (canonical alias for the property domain, ADR-287 precedent). Pattern matches EXPORTED declarations only — local component-internal const/function with same name is permitted (not a SSoT duplicate). ADR-314 Phase B.

unified-sharing-service.description: All sharing logic (token lifecycle, validation, access-count, revocation, listing) MUST route through UnifiedSharingService. Direct reads/writes on COLLECTIONS.SHARES or the literal 'shares' collection from other modules are forbidden. Re-implementation of createShare/validateShare/verifyPassword semantics outside src/services/sharing/ is a SSoT violation. FileShareService on file_shares remains operational (legacy) and is NOT subject to this module. Scheduled retirement in ADR-315 Phase M5. ADR-315 Phase M1.

channel-dispatch-service.description: All outbound share dispatches (email / telegram / whatsapp / messenger / instagram) MUST write an audit record in `share_dispatches` via ChannelDispatchService. Direct reads/writes on COLLECTIONS.SHARE_DISPATCHES from other modules are forbidden. Re-implementing dispatch-logging semantics outside src/services/sharing/ is a SSoT violation. ADR-315 Phase M3.

project-showcase-service.description: All server-side project showcase snapshot construction MUST route through buildProjectShowcaseSnapshot() in snapshot-builder.ts. Direct Firestore reads of the projects collection for showcase purposes, inline branding resolution, and re-implementation of the snapshot-to-PDF pipeline outside src/services/project-showcase/ are forbidden. PDF rendering via ProjectShowcasePDFService + ProjectShowcaseRenderer. Public API: GET /api/project-showcase/[token]. PDF API: POST /api/projects/[id]/showcase/pdf. ADR-316.

description-notes-card.description: Paired description+notes textareas on entity info tabs (storage, parking, future units/offices) MUST render via DescriptionNotesCard. Inline re-implementation of the pattern (Card wrapper with two Textarea fields bound to description+notes fields of a form state) is forbidden. Keeps the Card/Textarea layout and the StickyNote/violet styling consistent across entities. ADR-194.

modules._comment_adr314_phaseD1: === ADR-314 PHASE D.1 (2026-04-19): registry gap reduction — 6 high-export SSoT modules ===

message-utils.description: HTML sanitization with DOMPurify (XSS protection, OWASP A03:2021). Telegram/email tag allowlists, Email signature detection (ADR-072/ADR-073). Re-implementation of sanitizeHTML/sanitizeEmailHTML/formatMessageHTML/stripHTMLTags/detectEmailSignature/stripEmailSignature outside message-utils.ts = security hole (divergent sanitization rules = XSS bypass). ADR-314 Phase D.1.

firebase-admin.description: Firebase Admin SDK singleton accessors (ADR-077). Every server-side Firestore/Auth/Storage read/write MUST go through getAdminFirestore/getAdminAuth/getAdminStorage/getAdminBucket. Re-implementation of these accessors outside the module breaks the lazy singleton + credential chain (B64 -> JSON -> ADC) and risks double-initialisation / bucket drift. ADR-314 Phase D.1.

npv-engine.description: NPV / cash-flow math for the Interest Cost Calculator (ADR-234 SPEC-234E). Pure functions, zero side effects, runs on client + server. Re-implementation of calculateDiscountFactor/calculateNPV/calculateCashFlowAnalysis/calculateWACP/calculateTimeCost/calculateRecommendedPrice/calculateFullResult/buildComparisonScenarios outside this module forks the financial model (certainty multipliers, discount factors, time-cost formula). ADR-314 Phase D.1.

hedging-engine.description: Interest-rate hedging strategy math (Floating/Swap/Cap/Collar) for SPEC-242E Hedging Simulator (ADR-242). Pure math, zero side effects. Re-implementation of calculateFloatingCost/calculateSwapCost/calculateCapCost/calculateCollarCost/compareHedgingStrategies/findBreakEvenRate/risingScenario/flatScenario/decliningScenario forks the risk model. ADR-314 Phase D.1.

pagination.description: Firestore cursor-based pagination primitives — applyPagination/processPaginationResult/createPaginationState/updatePaginationState/calculatePaginationMetrics + InfiniteScrollPagination/PageBasedPagination classes. Every paginated Firestore read must go through this module to keep limit()/startAfter()/cursor semantics consistent (prevents N+1 queries, enforces MAX_PAGE_SIZE guard). ADR-314 Phase D.1.

rtl-utils.description: RTL (right-to-left) locale-aware helpers for bidirectional text and layout support. useRTL hook, containsRTLCharacters detection, bidiIsolate wrapping, formatRTLCurrency, sortRTL, generateRTLCSSVars + rtlClasses/rtlStyles/rtlIcon/rtlAnimation/rtlKeyboard/logicalProperties objects. Re-implementation fragments the RTL semantics (Arabic/Hebrew/Farsi) across the app. ADR-314 Phase D.1.
