modules._comment_adr327: === ADR-327 (2026-04-25): Quote Management & Comparison System SSoT ===

quote-entity.description: Quote entity + FSM SSoT (ADR-327). All quote CRUD goes through quote-service.ts. FORBIDDEN: addDoc() on quotes collection, inline status transitions, direct Firestore writes from API routes.

rfq-entity.description: RFQ entity + lifecycle SSoT (ADR-327). All RFQ CRUD goes through rfq-service.ts. FORBIDDEN: direct Firestore writes from API routes.

sourcing-event-entity.description: Sourcing Event entity + lifecycle SSoT (ADR-327 §17 Q31, Multi-Vendor extension 2026-04-29). Optional parent grouping N atomic single-trade RFQs into one logical multi-trade procurement package. status derived server-side from child RFQs via deriveSourcingEventStatus(). FORBIDDEN: direct addDoc on sourcing_events, inline status recomputation outside the service, parallel multi-trade RFQ schemas.

rfq-line-entity.description: RFQ line items as sub-collection rfqs/{rfqId}/lines/{lineId} (ADR-327 §17 Q29, Multi-Vendor extension 2026-04-29). Per Q29 (HYBRID Γ): BOQ-first snapshot + ad-hoc escape hatch. Lines are NEVER live-updated from the BOQ — snapshot semantics preserve quote integrity. Public portal projection via toPublicRfqLine() strips internal fields (unitPrice, boqItemId, source, companyId). FORBIDDEN: direct sub-collection writes outside rfq-line-service, inline addDoc on lines, exposing RfqLine.unitPrice to vendor portal.

trade-taxonomy.description: Trade taxonomy SSoT (ADR-327 §9). 32 trades / 8 groups. FORBIDDEN: hardcoded trade labels in components, inline trade lists, parallel trade enums. Use TRADE_CODES or TRADE_SEED_DATA.

vendor-portal.description: Vendor invite + portal token SSoT (ADR-327 §7, P3 fully implemented). createVendorInvite() is canonical — generates HMAC token, sends email/copy-link, writes to vendor_invites + vendor_invite_tokens. FORBIDDEN: direct addDoc on vendor_invites/vendor_invite_tokens, inline token generation outside vendor-invite-service.ts.

quote-comparison.description: Quote comparison + scoring engine SSoT (ADR-327 §8, P4 fully implemented). compareQuotes() scores all quotes per weights+templates; awardRfq() closes RFQ with winner + optional override-with-reason. COMPARISON_TEMPLATES defines 4 built-in templates. FORBIDDEN: inline scoring logic, hardcoded comparison weights, duplicate awardRfq flow.

po-auto-generation.description: Auto-PO generation from awarded quote SSoT (ADR-327 P5-ATOE). generatePoFromAwardedQuote() is the single entry point — maps quote lines to PO items, resolves VAT, calls createPO, stamps linkedPoId on the quote. FORBIDDEN: inline quote→PO mapping outside this service, duplicate award-to-PO flows.

pdf-rasterize.description: Server-side PDF→PNG rasterization SSoT (ADR-327 §6.7). rasterizePdfPages() is the single entry point — converts a PDF Buffer into per-page PNG Buffers using pdfjs-dist (legacy build) + @napi-rs/canvas. Use BEFORE sending PDFs to vision AIs (OpenAI Responses, Anthropic Vision) when documents have column-heavy or image-overlay layouts. FORBIDDEN: inline pdfjs.getDocument() in route handlers or services — always import this SSoT.

professional-role-routing.description: SSoT mapping: professional role string → DepartmentCode for L2 org-structure routing (ADR-326 Phase 6.2). mapRoleToDept() is the canonical lookup. FORBIDDEN: inline role→dept mapping outside this file.

ai-org-structure-tools.description: AI agentic tools for org structure (ADR-326 Phase 7). Canonical entry point for AI org-structure access. FORBIDDEN: AI tools that bypass resolveTenantNotificationEmail / resolveContactDepartmentEmail for routing; AI tools that read companies/{id}.settings.orgStructure or contacts/{id}.orgStructure outside the OrgStructureHandler; hardcoded department names in agentic prompts (must use DEPARTMENT_CODES).

onboarding-flow.description: Tenant onboarding state (ADR-326 Phase 8). Canonical read/write for companies/{id}.settings.onboarding. FORBIDDEN: direct Firestore writes to settings.onboarding outside onboarding-state-service; duplicating isBannerEligible / isRemindEligible logic outside onboarding-types.ts.

tabs-primitive.description: Tabs UI must use BaseTabs/StateTabs/RouteTabs from src/components/ui/navigation/. TabsContainer/ToolbarTabs/TabsOnlyTriggers (in TabsComponents.tsx) are deprecated aliases. AST-based ratchet CHECK 3.24 enforces this; this regex layer is a defense-in-depth grep against deprecated import lines (ADR-328).

no-navigation-flash.description: Hooks fetching list data MUST use createStaleCache + silentInitialFetch (ADR-300). PageContents MUST NOT gate render on isNamespaceReady or bare `if (loading)` returning <Loader2> — sister pages prove the canonical pattern is `if (loading && data.length === 0) return <PageLoadingState/>`. Regex-based ratchet CHECK 3.25 enforces this; this registry layer is a defense-in-depth grep against the most distinctive flash trigger (ADR-267 Phase H+3).

multi-combobox.description: MultiCombobox SSoT (ADR-331 D13). Centralized multi-select with search, chips, keyboard nav. Replaces inline rebuilds. Boy Scout post-Phase H: migrate 3 domain duplicates (PropertyMultiSelectByBuilding, FloorMultiSelectField, BOQEditorScopeSection).

floorplan-background-system.description: Floorplan Background System (ADR-340 Phase 7+8) — server-side persistence for the per-floor raster/PDF background entity. The `floorplan_backgrounds` and `floorplan_overlays` collections are domain-owned by `FloorplanBackgroundService` (Admin SDK CRUD with tenant isolation, immutables guard, transaction semantics) and `FloorplanCascadeDeleteService` (cascade across overlays + dxf_viewer_levels). Calibration polygon remap goes through `CalibrationRemapService.applyCalibration` (atomic batch, affine math). Direct client-side `addDoc/setDoc` on these collections is forbidden — every write must traverse the API route (`/api/floorplan-backgrounds`) which composes the services. Reader-side: client uses `floorplanBackgroundStore` (in-memory SSoT) hydrated by `useFloorplanBackgroundPersistence`. Legacy `pdfBackgroundStore` was removed in Phase 5/6 — `useState.*pdfBackground` is dead-code-shaped and any reintroduction outside the subsystem signals a regression.

data-url-utils.description: Data URL / base64 conversion utility — dataUrlToBlob. Canonical SSoT for converting base64 data-URLs to Blob objects (used by FloorFloorplanService for PDF canvas export). ADR-314 auto-discovered 2026-05-08.

overlay-renderer.description: Canonical multi-kind overlay renderer for FloorplanGallery (DXF + PDF + Image). SSoT for fit-and-center math (computeFitTransform), Y-UP world↔screen conversion, per-shape draw helpers (polygon/line/circle/arc/dimension/measurement/text), geometry-aware dispatch, and legacy polygon API. Replaces duplicate inline polygon-draw loops previously in floorplan-overlay-system.ts (DXF) and floorplan-pdf-overlay-renderer.ts (raster). Phase 9 STEP E split renderer-polygon SSoT into per-shape modules under `overlay-renderer/`. ADR-340 §3.6.

floorplan-overlay-gateway.description: Sole client write path for `floorplan_overlays` (multi-kind overlays — ADR-340 Phase 9). Blocks ad-hoc Firestore access to the collection from outside the gateway, the canonical API route+handlers, and the two server-side cascade services that legitimately need direct admin-SDK access (calibration-remap world-coord rewrites, cascade-delete on background/floor wipe). Pattern 1 forbids `COLLECTIONS.FLOORPLAN_OVERLAYS` references; pattern 2 forbids the raw `'floorplan_overlays'` literal in `.collection()`/`.doc()` calls (belt-and-suspenders with the firestore-collections module).

floorplan-overlay-types.description: Canonical TypeScript SSoT for multi-kind floorplan overlay shape (ADR-340 Phase 9). Blocks redeclarations of `FloorplanOverlay`, `OverlayGeometry`, `OverlayRole`, `OverlayLinked` outside the canonical types file + 3 legitimate sites: the polygon-only domain projection consumed by the DXF Viewer layering store, the provider-types re-export bridge (`type FloorplanOverlay = SharedFloorplanOverlay`), and the renderer re-export module. The pattern requires the identifier to be followed by `<`, `=`, or `{` (definition syntax) so inline type-import modifiers (`import { type FloorplanOverlay, ... }`) — comma-trailing — are NOT false positives. `OverlayStyle` is intentionally excluded — the DXF Viewer projection ships a structurally different `OverlayStyle` (lineWidth-based vs strokeWidth/dashed) and that's by design.

visibility-resolver.description: ADR-382 BIM visibility resolver SSoT — single intersection authority for V/G + Layer + Floor + Building (ANY-hides-wins). Consumed by all 7 BIM 2D renderers (Wall/Column/Beam/Slab/Stair/Opening/SlabOpening) and the 3D BimSceneLayer.sync() per-entity loop (Phase C). Forbids direct V/G category visibility reads (`objectStyles[cat]?.visible`) outside the resolver internals — bypasses the intersection and silently re-introduces the original 2D/3D parity bug (ADR-382 §1.1: hidden layer entities stayed visible in 3D because only V/G was consulted). resolveIsCategoryVisible() remains exported as a deprecated backward-compat wrapper for unit tests + legacy callers; new code must use resolveIsEntityVisible.

snap-engine.description: ProSnapEngineV2 single production singleton SSoT (ADR-378 Master Snap Architecture, 2026-05-27; supersedes ADR-040 Phase VIII narrow scope). Canonical access: `getGlobalSnapEngine()`. Scene-initialize lifecycle owned exclusively by `useGlobalSnapSceneSync` (called once from `SnapSceneSyncLeaf`, mounted once by CanvasSection — ADR-547 fix: the leaf subscribes the live SceneStore so in-session commits re-init the engine); other call sites forbidden. 26 sub-engines registered via SnapEngineRegistry (Orchestrator pattern — Revit/AutoCAD/ArchiCAD/BricsCAD/SketchUp/Vectorworks/Bentley industry convergence, 7/7). Forbidden: (1) `new ProSnapEngineV2(` outside canonical — parallel instance breaks ADR-040 perf invariants; (2) `new SnapManager(` legacy name; (3) `new SnapEngine(` — geo-canvas parallel 6-mode class deleted in ADR-378 Phase 4 (2026-05-27), reintroduction blocked; (4) `class AISnappingEngine` — conference demo dead code deleted in ADR-378 Phase 1 (2026-05-27), reintroduction blocked. Geo-canvas property floorplan tab now consumes the same engine via `useSnapEngine` façade + `parserResultToEntities` adapter (ADR-378 §3.1).

alignment-tracking.description: Alignment/POLAR tracking traces geometry SSoT (ADR-572). The dashed alignment-path / polar-ray inference lines (drawing hover, grip-drag, body-drag, 2-click MOVE, dimension create/grip, rotation) all funnel through ONE engine: resolveTrackingSnap (tracking-resolver.ts) → composeTrackingSnap (ambient-tracking-compose.ts), with ambient anchors from collectAmbientAlignmentAnchors (ambient-alignment-source.ts) and the live grip result held in setGripAlignmentTracking (GripAlignmentTrackingStore.ts). Three thin wrappers (resolveAlignmentTracking / resolveActionAlignmentTracking / resolveRotationTracking) call composeTrackingSnap — none has its own engine. Re-declaring any of these four core functions elsewhere reintroduces a parallel H/V/polar-ray + intersection/projection engine, the exact scatter ADR-572 audited and closed. Import the SSoT wrapper instead of re-implementing.

dxf-viewport-culling.description: Sole authority for per-entity viewport intersection in the DxfRenderer (ADR-040 Phase IX). Forbids alternate bbox / culling implementations.

modules._comment_dxf_text_engine: === DXF TEXT ENGINE MODULES — ADR-344 (bypass = text rendering violations) ===

mtext-parser.description: DXF MTEXT/TEXT entity → DxfTextNode AST (ADR-344 Layer 1). Handles both AcDbText and AcDbMText, STYLE table reading, MTEXT inline code tokenization for R12→R2018. Reimplementing tokenizeMText/parseMText inline bypasses version-conditional parsing branches and breaks round-trip integrity.

mtext-serializer.description: DxfTextNode AST → DXF MTEXT inline string (ADR-344 Layer 1). Emits correct entity type (TEXT vs MTEXT) based on node complexity, handles R12 graceful downgrade. Inline serializers drift from the spec and break interoperability.

font-engine.description: opentype.js font loading, glyph path cache, font substitution table (ADR-344 Layer 2). All font-to-glyph resolution must go through FontLoader — direct opentype.load() calls outside this module bypass the WeakMap cache, substitution table, and signed-URL fetch logic.

shx-parser.description: SHP stroke-vector playback for SHX font files (ADR-344 Layer 2, Q3). ~400-line TypeScript implementation of AutoCAD SHP format. Reimplementing parseShp/playbackShp inline produces glyph drift and breaks ISOCPEUR/romans rendering parity.

text-layout.description: UAX #14 line-breaking, column layout, stacking (\\S), 9-point attachment-point anchoring (ADR-344 Layer 3). Canonical layout pipeline for MTEXT. Inline layout bypasses UAX #14 and column/stacking spec, producing visual drift in multi-paragraph drawings.

text-renderer.description: Canvas 2D Path2D orchestrator for DXF text entities (ADR-344 Layer 3 + ADR-040 micro-leaf pattern). Coordinates glyph path, background-mask, and decoration rendering. Inline renderTextEntity/drawMTextEntity implementations violate ADR-040 bitmap-cache rules.

text-toolbar.description: Enterprise text ribbon toolbar (ADR-344 Layer 5). Canonical UI for font/size/color/bold/italic/justification/layer controls. All Radix Toolbar primitives per ADR-001. New text UI surfaces must compose from this component, not reimplement.

text-commands.description: ICommand implementations for text mutations: CreateText, UpdateTextStyle, UpdateTextGeometry, UpdateMTextParagraph, DeleteText, ReplaceAll/One (ADR-344 + ADR-031). All text mutations must flow through CommandHistory via these commands to preserve undo/redo and audit trail integration (ADR-195 Q12). Phase 6.A also centralizes the layer-edit guard (assertCanEditLayer) and the find/replace match engine here.

text-types.description: DxfColor discriminated union, MixedValue<T>, DxfTextNode AST, TextRun, TextRunStyle, TextJustification, AnnotationScale types (ADR-344 Appendix C). Type redeclaration scatters the DXF color model and breaks the ACI/TrueColor/ByLayer discriminated union.

text-collab.description: Yjs Y.Doc/Y.Text binding + y-websocket auth integration for collaborative DXF text editing (ADR-344 Q4). Direct Y.Doc construction outside this module bypasses Firebase Auth token validation and the session lifecycle managed by the collab service.

text-edit.description: TipTap (ProseMirror) JSON ↔ DxfTextNode serializer + canonical TipTap extension set (custom Marks for \O \H \W \Q \T \C/\c, custom inline Node for \S) for DXF inline codes (ADR-344 Phase 4). Direct Editor.create() outside this module bypasses the canonical DXF extension set; reimplementing the serializers risks asymmetric encoding/decoding and silent data loss on roundtrip.

text-templates.description: Hybrid text template system: TS built-in defaults + Firestore text_templates collection + placeholder resolver + management UI (ADR-344 Q5/Phase 7.B + 7.C + 7.D). Canonical CRUD service for the Firestore text_templates collection — all writes flow through text-template.service.ts (Admin SDK + EntityAuditService). Canonical placeholder resolver in text-engine/templates/resolver/: PLACEHOLDER_REGISTRY (variables.ts) is the SSoT for every supported {{namespace.key}}; resolver.ts performs pure substitution; scope-builder.ts is server-only and hydrates the scope from Firestore. Management UI in src/subapps/dxf-viewer/ui/text-templates/ (TextTemplateManager + list/preview/editor/delete-dialog + hooks); thin API routes in src/app/api/dxf/text-templates/ proxy to the service. Inline resolvers drift from the variable registry, direct setDoc/addDoc on text_templates bypasses the audit trail (CLAUDE.md N.11 baseline 3.17), and inline templates list/editor outside ui/text-templates/ duplicate the manager (Phase 7.D).

text-spell.description: nspell worker + el_GR (MPL-1.1 LibreOffice asset) / en_US (dictionary-en BSD-2) dictionaries + custom Firestore dictionary CRUD (ADR-344 Q6, Phase 8). Lazy-loaded on first text edit. Direct nspell / dictionary-en imports outside this module break the lazy-load strategy and duplicate dictionary loading. Custom dictionary writes go through /api/dxf/custom-dictionary (Admin SDK + audit + Zod).

text-draft.description: IndexedDB auto-save (30s debounce + beforeunload) + crash recovery service (ADR-344 Q15). Store key `dxf_text_draft_{companyId}_{drawingId}`. Direct IndexedDB writes using this key bypass the expiry policy (7-day auto-delete) and Yjs snapshot integration.

text-ai.description: NL → text command router using ADR-185 AIAnalysisProvider + ADR-156 voice hook (ADR-344 Q16). Maps natural language instructions to CreateTextCommand/UpdateTextStyleCommand/ReplaceAllTextCommand. Reimplementing the router inline creates duplicate AI intent mapping and bypasses CommandHistory.

text-snap.description: Canonical 8-point snap provider for DXF TEXT/MTEXT entities (ADR-344 Phase 6.B / Q21). Returns insertion + 4 corners + center + top-mid + bottom-mid, with entity rotation pre-applied. Reimplementing the snap-point taxonomy inline drifts from the SnapEngine priority ladder and breaks rotation handling.

bim-cursor-snap.description: ADR-514 «Ένας Εγκέφαλος Έλξης» — the ONE tool-agnostic BIM cursor placement-snap resolver (`resolveBimCursorSnap`). Given a (already-OSNAP-snapped) cursor + active toolKind ('wall'|'beam'|'column'|'point-only') it returns where the point lands + what it snapped to (discriminated union member-placement|column-placement|point). Commit AND preview of every BIM placement tool MUST call this ONE entry → preview ≡ commit by construction (column wired Φ2, wall+beam Φ3). It delegates to the existing Layer-2 resolvers; calling them directly from tool/preview code re-introduces the column-vs-wall/beam asymmetry the ADR eliminated. Forbidden: (1) re-declaring `resolveBimCursorSnap` outside canonical (parallel brain); (2) direct `resolveColumnFaceSnapFromTargets(` / `resolveMemberGhostSnapFromStore(` / `resolveBeamGhostSnapFromStore(` calls outside the canonical resolver files (column-face-snap, member-ghost-snap, beam-column-face-snap), the brain itself, and the member-endpoint POINT-snap leaf (framing/member-endpoint-snap — canonical END concern, shared wall+beam, re-exported by walls/wall-endpoint-snap). New BIM placement tools route through the brain, never the leaf resolvers.

text-grip.description: TextGripHandler + TextGripGeometry + DirectDistanceEntry — drag orchestrator, grip taxonomy/hit-testing, and AutoCAD-style numeric input for DXF TEXT/MTEXT (ADR-344 Phase 6.C / Q19). Commits flow through UpdateTextGeometryCommand + CommandHistory for undo/redo + audit. Reimplementing grip math or direct-distance buffering inline breaks the Command pipeline and the rotation/resize geometry contract.

viewport-system.description: ViewportStore + ViewportContext for annotative text scaling (ADR-344 Q11). Zustand store owns the active annotation scale name and viewport list. Canvas text renderer reads scale via getter per ADR-040 cardinal rules. Reimplementing viewport state creates stale-snapshot bugs in text rendering.

grip-rendering.description: All grip drawing (vertex squares, edge midpoint diamonds, close/selection rings) must go through UnifiedGripRenderer (ADR-048). renderGrip() for world-coord grips, renderEdgeMidpointGrip() for polygon edge diamonds, renderSquareRing() for overlay rings. Callers with screen coords pass identity worldToScreen. Forbidden: re-implementing getGripPath() and GRIP_PATH_CACHE outside the centralized system.

scene-layer-shape.description: SceneLayer (ADR-358 §5.1) construction must go through createSceneLayer() factory — never inline literal. Factory enforces 14-field shape contract (12 base + Q15 bimCategory scaffold + Q16 vpOverrides scaffold) and applies default-fill at boundary I/O. Inline `const x: SceneLayer = { ... }` literals bypass the contract and break the eventual Phase 9 cleanup that flips all fields to required. Legacy construction sites are allowlisted during the Phase 1→9 migration.

entity-layer-id-canonical.description: ADR-358 Phase 9D-5a: stable `layerId` (`lyr_<UUID-v4>`) is the canonical entity↔layer binding. Direct reads of `entity.layer` (the legacy display-name field) and writes via `.layer = '<name>'` literal are forbidden in active product code — use `resolveEntityLayerName(entity)` (id-first via LayerStore, legacy fallback) or `LayerStore.getLayer(entity.layerId)?.name`. Transitional readers + the BaseEntity interface itself + the DXF parse boundary remain allowlisted until Phase 9D-5b drops the legacy field outright. Ratchet, not zero-tolerance: existing reader sites baseline-tolerated until 9D-5b sweep.

unified-layer-store.description: LayerStore is the unified SSoT for SceneLayer + currentLayerId (ADR-358 §5.10 + Q3). Replaces the dual paradigm where DXF entity layers and overlay-manager `coreState.layers` (Region layers) were owned separately. Direct `RegionLayerObject` typed access and `coreState.layers` reads outside the legacy bridge files are forbidden for new code; Phase 5/9 will retire the bridge entirely. Co-existence ratchet, not zero-tolerance — current allowlist captures the transitional surface.

bim-category-scaffolding-no-active-use.description: ADR-358 Q15 SCAFFOLD: `SceneLayer.bimCategory` is a forward-compat field reserved for Future BIM Mode (Phase 11+). Reads and writes from product code are forbidden — the field exists only so DXF I/O can preserve XDATA `NestorBimCategory` round-trip without a future data-model refactor. DXF I/O round-trip allowlist: `dxf-layer-table-parser.ts` (read XDATA → bimCategory) + `dxf-layer-table-writer.ts` (write bimCategory → XDATA). Active product code use must come with a new ADR (likely ADR-362+) that defines BIM categories, IFC export pipeline, and Vectorworks-style dual-org.

vp-overrides-scaffolding-no-active-use.description: ADR-358 Q16 SCAFFOLD: `SceneLayer.vpOverrides` is a forward-compat field reserved for Future per-viewport overrides (VPLAYER, AutoCAD standard for 18+ years). Reads and writes from product code are forbidden — the field exists only so the DXF parser can preserve VP-override XDATA and the VP_FREEZE flag round-trip without a future data-model refactor. DXF I/O round-trip allowlist: `dxf-layer-table-parser.ts` + `dxf-layer-table-writer.ts`. Active product code use must come with a new ADR (Future paperspace).

dxf-layer-parser.description: ADR-358 §5.2 Phase 3A: the DXF LAYER table is parsed by a single entry point (`parseLayerTable()` in `dxf-layer-table-parser.ts`) with a mandatory LTYPE pre-pass (`parseLinetypeTable()` in `dxf-linetype-table-parser.ts`). Inline use of the Nestor XDATA AppId literals (AcCmTransparency, NestorAec, NestorLayerMeta, NestorLayerId, NestorBimCategory, NestorVpOverride) outside the parser + writer creates duplicate XDATA handling that breaks the §G15 round-trip guarantee. Zero-tolerance baseline — no legacy use exists. Phase 9C v2.13: added NestorLayerId for stable enterprise-id round-trip.

linetype-iso-catalog.description: ADR-358 §5.3 + §5.3.bis: the 8 ISO baseline linetype names (Continuous, Dashed, Hidden, Center, Phantom, DashDot, Border, Divide) are case-sensitive AutoCAD identifiers owned by `linetype-iso-catalog.ts`. Hardcoded `linetype:` / `linetype =` assignments to these literal names outside the catalog + registry + tests + factory bypass resolveLinetype() and break the .lin import / DXF-import round-trip story (Phase 3+). Use `getIsoLinetype(name)` or `resolveLinetype(name)`; for the default, use `DEFAULT_LINETYPE_NAME`. Pattern is narrow (assignment context only) to avoid false positives on snap names ('Center'), UI labels ('Border'), and other unrelated occurrences.

lineweight-iso-catalog.description: ADR-358 §5.3 + §5.3.ter: the 24 ISO lineweight values (mm) + 3 special sentinels (-3/-2/-1) are owned by `lineweight-iso-catalog.ts`. `as LineweightMm` casts and inline `lineweight:` / `lineweight =` numeric literal assignments outside the catalog + tests + entities.ts factory bypass the type contract and break the DXF group 370 round-trip story. Use `LINEWEIGHT_ISO_VALUES`, `LINEWEIGHT_SPECIAL`, `parseDxfCode370()`, or the `createSceneLayer()` factory. Baseline captures 3 legacy drawing-preview construction sites pending migration (Phase 5 render integration). The `default-lineweight-resolver` ratchet (system default 0.25mm hardcode ban) is deferred to Phase 5 too.

bim-line-weight-resolver.description: ADR-375 Phase A+B.1+B.2: BIM entity line weight + view settings SSoT (Revit-equivalent). resolveLineWeightPx(ctx) is the SOLE entry point for BIM entity ctx.lineWidth. useBimRenderSettingsStore is the SOLE SSoT for per-view BIM settings (drawingScale + viewRange + objectStyles). drawing-scale-store is a re-export shim — do NOT use it for new code. BIM renderers MUST call resolveLineWeightPx(); FORBIDDEN to use RENDER_LINE_WIDTHS or hardcoded px for main entity stroke.

opening-tag-layer.description: ADR-376 Phase A §4.7: reserved annotation layer `__system_opening_tags__` controlling global visibility of all opening tags. The layer ID literal MUST live only in the SSoT module + tests; consumers (OpeningRenderer, AnnotationsSection) interact through `isOpeningTagLayerVisible()` / `setOpeningTagLayerVisible()` / `useOpeningTagLayerVisible()`. Inline visibility state or re-declaration of the literal in another file fragments the SSoT and breaks the React useSyncExternalStore binding.

opening-renumber-service.description: ADR-376 Phase B.1 §7: pure renumber compute + Firestore fetch wrapper. `computeRenumberUpdates()` is the single source for renumber semantics — scope (current-floor / all-floors), per-kind isolation, chronological ordering by createdAt, `markIsManual` preservation. Consumers (RenumberOpeningsDialog, RenumberOpeningsHost, RenumberOpeningsCommand) MUST go through this module; inline reallocation, ad-hoc seq computation, or duplicate format SSoT (parseMarkSeq / formatMark) outside `opening-mark-service.ts` fragments the contract.

module-rounding.description: ADR-459 v16: SSoT για στρογγυλοποίηση διάστασης σε module βήμα (mm). `roundUpToModule` (tolerant-ceil — αφαιρεί MODULE_CEIL_EPSILON ώστε sub-ULP float dust να μην πηδά ολόκληρο module) + `roundDownToModule`. Αντικατέστησε 4 ταυτόσημα inline `Math.ceil(value/module)*module` (slab-sizing/member-sizing/column-sizing/suggest-pad-dimensions). Κάθε sizing/footing path MUST κάνει import από εδώ· inline re-definition ή raw module-ceiling fragments το dust-protection contract.

opening-boq-grouper.description: ADR-376 Phase B.2 §7: BOQ signature-group aggregation for openings. `computeOpeningSignature()` + `signatureKey()` + `signatureGroupBoqId()` + `groupBySignature()` + `buildOpeningGroupPayload()` + `compactMarkRange()` are the single source for how openings collapse into one BOQ row per (kind+width+height+sillHeight+openDirection) signature group per floorplan. Consumers (opening-boq-sync.ts, OpeningBOQHost) MUST route through this module; inline signature recomputation, ad-hoc grouping logic, or duplicate compactMarkRange implementations outside this file fragment the aggregation contract and produce split rows where the industry standard (Revit Schedule pattern 6/6 convergence) requires one.

opening-tag-drag-controller.description: ADR-376 Phase C.1 §7: FSM controller for draggable opening tags + leader geometry. `OpeningTagDragController` is the single owner of the idle/dragging state machine, tag hit-testing (`hitTestTag()`), world-center computation (`tagWorldCenter()`), offset persistence (`getOffsetOrZero()`), and screen-to-world delta conversion (`screenDeltaToWorldDelta()`). Hit-box constants (`TAG_HIT_HALF_WIDTH_PX`, `TAG_HIT_HALF_HEIGHT_PX`, `LEADER_MIN_SCREEN_DISTANCE_PX`) MUST NOT be re-declared — consumers (canvas leaf, OpeningTagRenderer) import from this module. Inline FSM re-implementation or duplicate hit-box constants produce divergent drag behaviour and break the RAF-coalesced scene patch pipeline (ADR-040 §4 micro-leaf pattern).

opening-tag-style.description: ADR-376 Phase C.2 §7: per-project opening tag style singleton (fontSize, borderWidth, leaderStyle, pillBgColor, leaderColor, leaderVisible). `getOpeningTagStyleService()` is the single entry point for style mutations and subscriber registration; `getCurrentOpeningTagStyle()` is the single getter for render paths (OpeningTagRenderer, OpeningTagStyleDialog preview). `OPENING_TAG_STYLE_DEFAULTS` is the canonical baseline — no inline defaults elsewhere. `resolveOpeningTagStyle()` owns clamping + validation; consumers MUST NOT re-implement field validation. Direct construction of `OpeningTagStyleServiceImpl` outside the singleton factory is forbidden. Debounce (200ms) is owned by the service; callers MUST NOT add their own debounce on top.

resolve-entity-style.description: ADR-358 §G7 Phase 6: the ByLayer/ByBlock/Concrete cascade — entity → block → layer → system default for color + linetype + lineweight + transparency — is owned by `resolve-entity-style.ts`. Calls to `resolveEntityStyle()` must live in the centralised renderer path (`DxfRenderer.resolveStyleForRender`) so that `AdminLayerManager` edits propagate live to canvas and snapshot/print/export paths share the same resolution semantics. Bypass call sites flatten styles ad-hoc and break the §G7 LIVE pipeline. Property panels reading provenance for inspection are allowlisted.

current-layer-picker.description: ADR-358 §5.5.bis Q8 Phase 7 (+ ADR-510 Φ4): the user-initiated current-layer change flow (permission gate + toast + recent FIFO + per-project/per-level persistence) is owned by a single SSoT — the effect-free `useCurrentLayerChange` action hook (the ONE code path, shared by the `CurrentLayerPicker` popover AND the ribbon line-tool «Επίπεδο» combobox) plus the `useCurrentLayerPickerState` host hook. Direct calls to `setCurrentLayerId` / `pushRecentLayer` / `setRecentLayerIds` outside the LayerStore SSoT and these two hooks fragment that flow. Component imports outside the two ratcheted host mount points (CadStatusBar status-bar variant, RibbonPanel ribbon variant) duplicate the picker UI. Zero-tolerance baseline at landing.

layer-name-strict-validation.description: ADR-358 §5.6 Q9 (Strict AutoCAD parity) — `validateLayerName()` is the single source of truth for SceneLayer.name validation. 7 rules (EMPTY / WHITESPACE_ONLY / LEADING_TRAILING_WS / TOO_LONG / INVALID_CHARS / RESERVED Layer-0 / DUPLICATE) + Layer-0 immutable hardening (can never be renamed, deleted, or reused). All consumers (UI rename inputs, LayerOperationsService.createLayer / renameLayer, DXF parser fallback) must call this function rather than re-implementing checks; duplicate validator implementations create defender-hierarchy drift (UI accepts but service rejects, or vice versa) and break AutoCAD parity. Phase 9A landed the pure validator + 45 tests; Phase 9B wires it into LayerOperationsService; Phase 9G wires UI real-time hints + Layer 0 system badge.

layer-isolate-system.description: ADR-358 §5.6.bis Q10 (FULL Enterprise Configurable) — `layer-isolate-resolver.ts` is the single source of truth for resolving Layer Isolate UX settings (mode + dim opacity %). The unisolate snapshot lives in `LayerStore.unisolateSnapshot` and MUST be touched only by Phase 10 layer commands (`core/commands/layer/**`). Hardcoded `dimOpacityPercent` literals bypass the project/user cascade and create drift between Settings panel + render pipeline + commands. Phase 10 ratchet: forbids `unisolateSnapshot` direct reads/writes outside the LayerStore + layer commands and forbids hardcoded numeric `dimOpacityPercent` literals outside the resolver/defaults.

quick-style-store.description: ADR-357 Phase 17 — `QuickStyleStore` is the SSoT for per-session drawing style overrides (lineweightMm / linetypeName / colorMode+colorAci+colorTrueColor). Applied by `completeEntity()` to newly created entities when overrides are non-ByLayer. Direct reads/writes of `localStorage` keys `dxf:quickStyle.*` outside this store are forbidden (must use exported setters/getters).

layer-state-system._phase13b_note: Phase 13B.1 added cross-project templates SSoT: types/layer-state-template.ts + services/dxf-layer-state-template.service.ts. Construction factories `createLayerStateTemplate` / `createDxfTemplateCategory` are forbidden outside the types + service + their tests. Phase 13B.2 added the store extension (`saveCurrentAsTemplate` / `importTemplateAsState` / `searchTemplateSummaries`) + service injection (`setLayerStateTemplateService`) + orchestrator hook `useLayerStateTemplates` — these symbols are forbidden outside the store + hook + their tests.
layer-state-system.description: ADR-358 §5.9 Q12 (FULL Enterprise). `LayerStateStore.ts` is the SSoT for saved LayerState runtime state. `layer-state-persistence.ts` owns the localStorage Firestore-shaped pub/sub layer. `types/layer-state.ts` exports `createLayerState` / `createLayerStateEntry` factories — direct `LayerState` literal construction outside this allowlist is forbidden. `RestoreLayerStateCommand` is the ONLY caller of `applyLayerSnapshotEntries`. Phase 13A 2026-05-17: extended with `.las` parser/exporter SSoT — `las-parser.ts` and `las-exporter.ts` are the ONLY modules permitted to parse / serialize `.las` content (forbiddenPatterns `parseLasContent` + `serializeLasContent`). Phase 13B.2 2026-05-17: the store API additions for cross-project templates (`saveCurrentAsTemplate`, `importTemplateAsState`, `searchTemplateSummaries`, `setLayerStateTemplateService`) are reachable only through `useLayerStateTemplates`.

layer-filter-engine.description: ADR-358 §5.7.bis Q11 (FULL Enterprise Δ + GOL) — `layer-filter-engine.ts` is the SSoT for evaluating `LayerFilter` (group + properties with recursive AND/OR rulesets). `layer-smart-filters.ts` owns the deterministic Smart filter set derived from a LayerStore snapshot. `layer-filter-validation.ts` owns the defensive JSON validation used by both import + Firestore hydrate. Phase 11 ratchet: forbids `applyLayerFilter` / `getSmartFilters` / `validateLayerFilterJson` invocations outside the allowlist files.

bim-entities.description: BIM entity base types (ADR-363): BimEntity generic, BimValidation, BimQuantityTakeoff, SoftLock, Point3D. All BIM entity interfaces extend BimEntity — no re-implementation of these types elsewhere.

bim-id-prefix.description: BIM entity ID prefixes (ADR-363): WALL='wall', OPENING='opening', SLAB='slab', SLAB_OPENING='slbopn', COLUMN='col', BEAM='beam', BIM_PRESET='bpst', BIM_MATERIAL='bmat'. All BIM IDs generated via enterprise-id-convenience.ts generateWallId() etc — no inline UUID, no hardcoded prefix strings.

xline-mode-store.description: XLine construction mode SSoT (ADR-359 Phase 2): XLineModeStore owns mode, angleValue, offsetDistance, sourceEntityId, bisect points. Three surfaces (keyboard/status-bar/context-menu) all read/write via getMode()/setMode()/subscribe(). Forbids direct xlineMode state access outside the store.

bim-cascade-resolver.description: BIM host→hosted entity discovery (ADR-363 Phase 7A): all wall→opening and slab→slab-opening orphan / cascade sweeps must go through findHostedOpenings / findHostedSlabOpenings / expandSelectionForDelete / expandSelectionForMove. Forbids inline `entities.filter(isOpeningEntity)…wallId.has(…)` patterns and equivalent slab→slab-opening inline sweeps outside the canonical module.

bim-to-boq-bridge.description: BIM→BOQ auto-feed bridge (ADR-363 Phase 6+): all BIM entity saves must trigger BimToBoqBridge.upsertBoqItem() — no direct boqService.upsertItem() calls from BIM entity handlers, no inline BIM→ATOE mapping tables outside BimToBoqBridge.

thermal-envelope.description: ADR-396 P7 (2026-05-29): SSoT for ETICS thermal-envelope per-element application + BOQ. Per-element envelopeLayer/revealInsulation generation MUST go through envelope-element-applicator.ts; per-zone+floor BOQ rows (boq_env_<floorId>_<zone>) MUST be built only by envelope-boq-sync.ts; the EnvelopeLayer/ThermalEnvelopeSpec Zod schemas live only in thermal-envelope.schemas.ts. Forbidden: re-declaring EnvelopeLayerSchema or constructing boq_env_ rows outside these files.

escape-command-bus.description: All Escape-key dispatch in the DXF viewer MUST go through escapeBus.register() or the useEscapeHandler() hook (ADR-364). Direct window/document keydown listeners that branch on e.key === 'Escape' are forbidden outside the escape-bus folder. Allowlist contains the three legacy capture hooks that retain non-ESC keys + the registrations shim + tooling that needs to synthesize a fake Escape event for Strategy-handler routing.

bim-audit-helpers.description: ADR-379 (2026-05-27) + ADR-380 (2026-05-27): SSoT for building AuditFieldChange[] payloads from BIM entity snapshots — wall/column/slab/beam/opening (ADR-379) + stair/slab-opening (ADR-380). Three canonical builders: buildBimCreationChanges (oldDoc={}), buildBimUpdateChanges (prev→next diff via diffTrackedFields), buildBimDeletionChanges (reverse diff, oldValue→null). Replaces 7 copy-pasted buildXxxChanges() functions that emitted only {field:'kind'} placeholders. All BIM audit-clients MUST consume these helpers — forbidden patterns block any re-introduction of the legacy per-type builders.

bim-renderer-finalize.description: ADR-363 Boy Scout N.0.2 (2026-05-19): `BaseEntityRenderer.finalizeRender(entity, options)` is the SSoT for grip rendering in BIM renderers. The inline `if (options.grips) { this.renderGrips(entity, options); }` pattern was copy-pasted into 7 BIM renderers (WallRenderer, ColumnRenderer, BeamRenderer, OpeningRenderer, SlabOpeningRenderer, SlabRenderer, StairRenderer) and centralized in this session. All BIM renderers MUST call `this.finalizeRender(entity, options)` — no inline renderGrips calls. Note: `finalizeRendering` (with -ing suffix) is the older method used by TextRenderer (grips + cleanup); BIM renderers use the shorter `finalizeRender` (grips only).

bim-schedule.description: ADR-363 §6 Phase 8 (2026-05-19): BIM Schedule Export system — types, filters, schedule-presets, schedule-builder, exporters (CSV/xlsx/PDF). All BIM schedule generation MUST go through bim/schedule/. Forbidden: re-implementing ScheduleBuilder, CsvScheduleExporter, XlsxScheduleExporter, PdfScheduleExporter, or BimScheduleFilterEngine outside the canonical tree.

tailwind-hardcoded-palette.description: ADR-365 (2026-05-19): Tailwind raw palette utilities (bg|text|border|ring|fill|stroke)-(slate|gray|zinc|neutral|stone|red|orange|amber|yellow|lime|green|emerald|teal|cyan|sky|blue|indigo|violet|purple|fuchsia|pink|rose)-(50..950) and their dark:/hover:/focus:/active:/group-hover:/peer-hover: variants are FORBIDDEN in consumer files. Use semantic tokens from COLOR_BRIDGE (src/design-system/color-bridge.ts), semanticColors (src/design-system/semantics/colors.ts), or shadcn tokens (muted/accent/destructive/primary/ring/background/card/foreground/muted-foreground). For status states use CSS vars: bg-[hsl(var(--bg-success|error|warning|info))]. Dark-mode prefix is unnecessary on consumer files — semantic tokens are theme-aware via CSS vars. Enforced by CHECK 3.26 (scripts/check-tailwind-palette-ratchet.js). Baseline: .tailwind-palette-baseline.json. Phase 0 ratchet only — per-domain migration (Phases 1-8) drives baseline → 0.

entity-renderer-viewport-source.description: ADR-362 Round 4.1 (2026-05-19): All entity renderers MUST resolve viewport pixels via `BaseEntityRenderer.getViewport()` (CSS pixels via `ctx.canvas.getBoundingClientRect()`), not via the backing-store `ctx.canvas.width / height` (DPR-scaled). Mixing the two for `worldToScreen` / `screenToWorld` / hit-testing causes silent mis-placement under non-100% browser zoom or HiDPI (DPR ≠ 1) — the Round 4 DIM text/line and center-mark mis-placement incident. Canonical pattern: `const rect = ctx.canvas.getBoundingClientRect(); width: rect.width || ctx.canvas.width` (fallback only). Orchestrators (DxfRenderer/LayerCanvas) still call `ctx.setTransform(dpr, ...)` and `ctx.clearRect(0, 0, ctx.canvas.width, ctx.canvas.height)` — positional args, not `width:` object keys, so unaffected.

bim-readonly-render.description: ADR-370 (2026-05-20): Read-only BIM entity visualization inside the Properties floorplan tab. The façade `renderBimEntitiesToCanvas()` instantiates the existing DXF Viewer BIM renderers (Wall/Slab/Beam/Column/Opening/SlabOpening) and dispatches them onto the FloorplanGallery canvas using a synthetic ViewTransform built by `buildBimViewTransform()`. Forbidden: re-implementing BIM rendering pipelines (BaseEntityRenderer subclasses, BIM scene composition) outside the DXF Viewer subapp, or instantiating BIM renderers (`new WallRenderer(...)`, `new SlabRenderer(...)`, etc.) outside this façade or the DXF Viewer canvas pipeline. Geometry hydration (docs → entities) must reuse `computeXxxGeometry` SSoT functions via `bim-readonly-hydration.ts` — no duplicate `docToEntity` adapters.

keyboard-shortcuts-3d.description: ADR-366 Phase 4.4 / A.6 (2026-05-21): All BIM 3D keyboard shortcut definitions and dispatch must live under `bim-3d/shortcuts/`. Forbidden: ad-hoc `event.code === 'Numpad...'` matching outside the canonical dispatcher (event matching MUST go through `matchView3DShortcut()` / `matchesShortcutDef()`), inline `view3d:<id>` action string literals outside the SSoT (use `view3dAction(view)` constructor), and re-implementations of the auto-switch/selection-aware-F branching outside `shortcut-dispatcher.ts`. New 3D shortcuts add entries to `ALL_VIEW_3D_SHORTCUTS`; new dispatch branches modify `dispatchShortcut()`.

frame-scheduler-master-raf.description: ADR-040 Phase XXIII + ADR-366 Phase 4.2 (2026-05-27): UnifiedFrameScheduler is the sole master `requestAnimationFrame` for the DXF viewer subapp. The BIM 3D scene MUST be ticked via `UnifiedFrameScheduler.register('bim-3d-scene', …)` and provide a dirty-check (`isSceneDirty()`); it MUST NOT call `requestAnimationFrame` itself. Industry alignment 4/4 — Forge Viewer SDK / Three.js Editor / iModel.js / AutoCAD Web. Forbidden patterns target the recurring-loop signature `this.rafHandle = requestAnimationFrame(animate)` and the recursive `this.rafHandle = requestAnimationFrame((ts) => …)` shape that the scheduler itself uses, with allowlists for the scheduler internals and tool-preview one-shots (those rAFs are gated by tool-active state, not persistent during wheel-zoom).

command-merge-window.description: ADR-507 §8 (2026-06-21): The 500ms command-merge time predicate (Autodesk/Adobe parity) is owned by `isWithinMergeWindow()` in merge-window.ts. Forbidden: inline `DEFAULT_MERGE_CONFIG.mergeTimeWindow` time-window comparisons in any command — call `isWithinMergeWindow(this, other)` so the policy lives in ONE place.

snapshot-geometry-strip.description: ADR-507 §8 (2026-06-21): Stripping identity fields (id/layer/visible, optionally type) from an entity snapshot to produce geometry-to-restore is owned by `geometryFromSnapshot()` (pass `{ excludeType: true }` for geometry-only restores like Extend/Trim). Forbidden: inline `{ id: _id, layer: _layer, visible: _… , ...rest }` snapshot-strip destructures in commands — call geometryFromSnapshot instead.

comma-normalize.description: ADR-397/513 (2026-07-06): el-GR comma→dot decimal normalization for the DXF viewer is owned by normalizeNumber() in systems/dynamic-input/utils/number.ts (canonical, `/,/g` superset — covers single decimals AND multi-comma arithmetic expressions fed to evalExpr in the RadialCommandRing). Forbidden: inline `.replace(',', '.')` / `.replace(/,/g, '.')` — import normalizeNumber instead. CROSS-DOMAIN NOTE: the app-level currency/decimal SSoT lives in src/lib/number/ — locale-number.ts (ADR-576: normalizeDecimalString + parseLocaleNumber, locale-aware building blocks) plus the thin greek-decimal.ts wrapper. It interprets separators for the currency domain (last-separator = decimal, thousands grouping stripped) and returns number|null / canonical string — a DIFFERENT contract from the DXF viewer, so both files are allowlisted here (their own SSoT), not violations. The 4 former app consumers (building-code / procurement / property-details / accounting csv-import) were migrated to these building blocks on 2026-07-06 (ADR-576) and no longer hand-roll comma-replace. Per-keystroke applyTypedAngleKey (single-char inline buffer, ADR-397 §15) is a different level and is not full-string normalization.
